vulns.title: Apache ActiveMQ Improper Input Validation Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia Remote Code Execution (RCE)
vulns.technicalSummary: Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia Remote Code Execution (RCE)
vulns.breakdown: Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia Remote Code Execution (RCE)
vulns.whatBroke: Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia Remote Code Execution (RCE)
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at K3ysTr0K3R/CVE-2026-34197.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/K3ysTr0K3R/CVE-2026-34197
cd CVE-2026-34197
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: initial access through exposed ActiveMQ
credential access from service context
webshell or command execution follow-up
internal pivot from an internet-facing control point
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, contain an authentication bypass vulnerability could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass.
vulns.technicalSummary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, contain an authentication bypass vulnerability could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass.

vulns.whatBroke: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, contain an authentication bypass vulnerability could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at anuththara2007-W/CVE-2026-20127-Exploit-Extension.

cd CVE-2026-20127-Exploit-Extension
ruby <script>.rb --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation

vulns.title: Microsoft and Adobe Patch Tuesday, July 2026 Security Update Review
vulns.summary: Proof of concept for an MSMQ remote-read integer overflow
vulns.technicalSummary: Microsoft’s July 2026 Patch Tuesday delivers security updates for a broad range of products and services, including several vulnerabilities that pose significant risks to enterprise environments. As attackers continue to target.
vulns.breakdown: Microsoft’s July 2026 Patch Tuesday delivers security updates for a broad range of products and services, including several vulnerabilities that pose significant risks to enterprise environments. As attackers continue to target.
vulns.whatBroke: Proof of concept for an MSMQ remote-read integer overflow
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at DavidCarliez/CVE-2026-54992-PoC.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/DavidCarliez/CVE-2026-54992-PoC
cd CVE-2026-54992-PoC
make
./<compiled-poc> <authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation

internal pivot from an internet-facing control point

vulns.title: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances Server-Side Request Forgery Vulnerability
vulns.summary: This repo contains a proof-of-concept exploit for. It establishes non-root remote code execution on SonicWall SMA 1000 by implementing the Erlang protocol expected by localhost:1050 and tunneling it through the websocket for.
vulns.technicalSummary: This repo contains a proof-of-concept exploit for. It establishes non-root remote code execution on SonicWall SMA 1000 by implementing the Erlang protocol expected by localhost:1050 and tunneling it through the websocket for.
